tool_name: Metallic Expansion Joint Sizing — Axial Displacement, Spring Rate & Fatigue Cycle Life

inputs.name: Pipe Run Length Between Fixed Anchors (L_pipe)
Pipe Material — Thermal Expansion Coefficient (α)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Design Internal Pressure (P_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Number of Convolutions (Bellows Corrugations)
inputs.note: More convolutions → lower spring rate, higher cycle life, lower stress per cycle
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Bellows Material Yield Strength at Temperature (S_y)
inputs.note: SS316L at 20°C → 205 MPa; at 400°C → 140 MPa; Inconel 625 → 415 MPa
inputs.uncertainty_source: JCSS PMC Part 3 §25.2 Table 25.1 — Yield/ultimate strength (steel): CoV=0.07

formulas: delta_T = T_operating_c - T_install_c; delta_axial_mm = pipe_material * 1e-6 * delta_T * pipe_length_m * 1000   // [mm] Thermal Axial Displacement (δ_axial) Reference: δ = α·ΔT·L — ASME B31.3 §319.4; EJMA 10th Ed. §2.1

delta_req_mm = delta_axial_mm * 1.25   // [mm] Required Axial Movement Capacity per Joint Reference: EJMA §2.2 — 25% safety margin on calculated displacement for uncertainty in anchor loads and fabrication tolerance

D_m_mm = pipe_OD_mm + 30; t_bellow_mm = 0.8; f_ax_n_mm = 1.7 * Math.pow(D_m_mm, 0.5) * bellows_material_yield_mpa * Math.pow(t_bellow_mm, 3) / (Math.pow(30, 3) * num_convolutions)   // [N/mm] Approximate Bellows Axial Spring Rate (f_ax) Reference: EJMA 10th Ed. §5.3 — simplified spring rate; f = 1.7·D_m^0.5·E·t³/(h³·n); h=convolution height assumed 30 mm; t=0.8 mm

F_pressure_kn = design_pressure_bar * 1e5 * Math.PI/4 * Math.pow(pipe_OD_mm/1000, 2) / 1000; F_spring_kn = f_ax_n_mm * delta_axial_mm / 1000; F_anchor_kn = F_pressure_kn + F_spring_kn   // [kN] Axial Force Transmitted to Anchors (F_anchor) Reference: EJMA §4.1 — anchor load = pressure thrust + spring force; pressure thrust = P·A_pipe

sigma_m_mpa = 3 * f_ax_n_mm * delta_req_mm / (num_convolutions * Math.PI * pipe_OD_mm * 0.8)   // [MPa] Meridional Bending Stress in Convolution (σ_m) — Fatigue Driver Reference: EJMA §5.5 — simplified meridional stress from axial deflection

stress_ratio = sigma_m_mpa / bellows_material_yield_mpa; N_life = (stress_ratio < 0.3) ? 100000 : (stress_ratio < 0.5) ? 50000 / stress_ratio : (stress_ratio < 0.8) ? 10000 / Math.pow(stress_ratio, 3) : 1000 / Math.pow(stress_ratio, 5)   // [cycles] Estimated Fatigue Cycle Life (N_life) Reference: EJMA Appendix C fatigue curve — SS316L; N proportional to (S_y/σ_m)^k; indicative only

V1.error_msg: Required displacement exceeds convolution capacity (15 mm/conv limit): add convolutions, use two expansion joints, or reduce anchor spacing

V2.error_msg: Meridional stress > 80% yield: very short fatigue life — increase convolution count or specify lower spring rate bellows with larger crown height

V3.error_msg: Fatigue life < 1000 cycles: bellows will fail rapidly — review displacement calculation; consider tied or hinged joint if lateral movement is present

smart_warnings.message: Anchor load > 500 kN: large main anchors required — pressure thrust on unrestrained bellows is major structural load; evaluate tied universal expansion joint to eliminate thrust
Carbon steel above 450°C: creep domain — ASME B31.3 requires time-dependent allowable stress; verify bellows material rating at temperature
High pressure with few convolutions: instability risk — EJMA §7 squirm (in-plane and column) must be checked; add internal sleeve for pressure > 40 bar
Large displacement with few convolutions: excessive stress per convolution — minimum 6 convolutions for δ > 50 mm per EJMA design practice
Fatigue life < 10,000 cycles: limited life joint — document replacement interval in maintenance plan; N_life cycles at operating temperature ΔT per EJMA C

fmea.effect: Required displacement exceeds convolution capacity (15 mm/conv limit): add convolutions, use two expansion joints, or reduce anchor spacing
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Fatigue life < 1000 cycles: bellows will fail rapidly — review displacement calculation; consider tied or hinged joint if lateral movement is present
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: Anchor load > 500 kN: large main anchors required — pressure thrust on unrestrained bellows is major structural load; evaluate tied universal expansion joint to eliminate thrust
Carbon steel above 450°C: creep domain — ASME B31.3 requires time-dependent allowable stress; verify bellows material rating at temperature
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_operating_c > 450 && pipe_material === 11.7

fmea.failureMode: High pressure with few convolutions: instability risk — EJMA §7 squirm (in-plane and column) must be checked; add internal sleeve for pressure > 40 bar
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: design_pressure_bar > 40 && num_convolutions < 3

fmea.failureMode: Large displacement with few convolutions: excessive stress per convolution — minimum 6 convolutions for δ > 50 mm per EJMA design practice
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: delta_req_mm > 50 && num_convolutions < 6

fmea.failureMode: Fatigue life < 10,000 cycles: limited life joint — document replacement interval in maintenance plan; N_life cycles at operating temperature ΔT per EJMA C
